quote:
Don't reflect, don't think, don't speculate.
Don't meditate, don't analyze - rest naturally.
Karma, though nothing in itself, is like an artist creating everything.

Thus with free, untrammeled mind,
Put on an ever-smiling countenance.
Rid yourselves of scowling, wrathful frowns,
And be a true and honest friend to all.

My actions are my only true belongings.
I cannot escape the consequences of my actions.
My actions are the ground upon which I stand.
source: Understanding Our Mind: 50 Verses on Buddhist Psychology.

quote:
If there is self, other is cognized.
From the pair of self and other come clinging and anger.
The connections with these two
produce all faults.
